rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HOWARD ROBIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents Rebecca Schumacher And Guido Piccininis Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate Litigation And Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 15, 2020 line 1. RESPONDENTs REBECCA SCHUMACHER and GUIDO PICCINNI's Motion To Consolidate Litigation And Arbitration READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Respondent's Motion to Consolidate Litigation and Arbitration is GRANTED. Non-opposition filed.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SYLVIA GILLESPIE VS. KIMO INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 15, 2020 line 5. DEFENDANT KIMO INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant Kimo Inc's Demurer to Complaint is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a purported third-party beneficiary is a proper defendant in a cause of action for rescission of a contract to which the defendant is not a party. Demurrer is otherwise OVERRULED. Causes of action for rescission of the listing agreement (entered into by the parties per paragraph 19 of the complaint) and elder abuse (property taken as there is effectively a cloud on Plaintiff's title) are sufficiently alleged. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SYLVIA GILLESPIE VS. KIMO INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 15, 2020 line 5. DEFENDANT KIMO INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendants' Kimo Inc's Motion to Strike Complaint is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as follows: (1) allegations pertaining to Spanish translation are stricken as irrelevant with leave to amend for Plaintiff to either allege facts supporting applicability of CCC 1632 or to allege facts referenced in opposition 3:26-4:1; (2) reference to equitable indemnity in the second cause of action is stricken with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege a separate cause of action and facts supporting alternative remedy of equitable indemnity. Motion is otherwise denied. Request for punitive damages and attorney's fees is sufficiently alleged at this stage of the litigation. = (501/CFH)
